selected_important_turn.text: I was hoping to pick up a lot of wood. It has rained around here and the wood is soggy in the forest.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn escalates competition over the scarce high-value item (firewood) right after the opponent declared a strong need for it. By matching their pro-self information with another pro-self claim, it helps lock the negotiation into a zero-sum frame and raises the stakes for subsequent bargaining.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is largely declarative and competitive rather than constructive. It amplifies conflict without exploring tradeoffs or the opponent's flexibility. Because the opponent had just revealed a strong preference, this was a prime moment to probe for specifics or open integrative options rather than reinforce contestation.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the competitive claim and pivot to information-gathering or tradeoff-building: acknowledge the other's need, ask how much wood they actually need or how flexible they are, or offer a small concession in exchange for partial wood. Avoid broad maximal claims and frame your interest as negotiable to create room for integrative solutions.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase (if replaced with probing/tradeoff language — opens integrative options)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (by enabling a more balanced exchange rather than winner-take-all posturing)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces escalation and creates pathways to agreement)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less antagonistic tone will build trust and rapport)

value2reason.Low: I know how to filter from natural sources.
value2reason.Medium: I have hunting skills and some pre made food.
value2reason.High: I do not have tools to cut trees and I need fire to cook.

value2reason.Low: We can drink water from the nearby river although I think bottled water tastes better.
value2reason.Medium: We have good supply of food and we have snacks too. We can fish and roast the fish in the fire. It will a great experience.
value2reason.High: I promised my kids that we are going to make smores and they can stay up all night telling horror stories to each other. our current supply might only last a few hours.

dialogue.text: Hello my friend! Are you ready to go camping?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Getting there, just need some more supplies to be safe.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Got you! I really need firewood to make smores and I don't think we have enough to last the whole night!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I was hoping to pick up a lot of wood. It has rained around here and the wood is soggy in the forest.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand you need firewood. How much do you really need, and are you flexible on the amount? I’d be willing to offer some food in exchange for a portion of the firewood if that works for you.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need all 3 firewood packages for the smores and stories. I’m flexible on food and water. Maybe you can take 1 food and 1 water in exchange? That would give you 4 points, while I get 15 for the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see. If you need all 3 firewood packages, I can offer 2 food and 1 water in exchange. That would give you 15 points, and I’d get 8 points for the remaining 1 food and 2 water. Let’s see if that works for you.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need all 3 firewood packages for the smores and stories. I’m flexible on food and water. Maybe you can take 1 food and 1 water in exchange? That would give you 4 points, while I get 15 for the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see you really need all the firewood. How about this: I’ll take 2 food and 1 water, and you take the 3 firewood. That way, you get your priority items, and I get what I need for cooking. Let’s agree on that?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. I’ll take the 3 firewood packages, and you can have 2 food and 1 water. Let’s confirm this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed! I’ll take 2 food and 1 water, and you have the 3 firewood. Safe camping!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
